title: ISic004291. I.Sicily inscription 004291
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/VDAQH5D2
1Δ̣..[---]
2ΔΤΤΤ

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/VDAQH5D2
1.··[---]
2ΔΤΤΤ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of I.Lipara;
  • 2: Δ followed by symbol equivalent to Latin 'b', followed by T followed by T and B in combination, followed by T

translation

commentary

The 'b' symbol recalls the acrophonic numeral '10' in Entella tablet V (ISic030298) and other Sicilian documents such as the gold phiale from the area of Himera. However, given that this is construed as a form of archaic delta, serving for 10, it is difficult to explain why it occurs in combination with standard delta. In such a sequence, the T could be understood as representing Talent, and the b in combination with the T to signify 10 talents. But the exact resolution is far from obvious, only that it seems likely that this is a numeral sequence of some description.

bibliography

  •   Bernabó-Brea  Cavalier  Campagna  2003 794 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000541
Line 1 Interlineation line 1 to 2
